explanationZhTw: 在這裡不只是「消失」，也有被徹底抹去、突然不見的語氣。
generatedExample: After the final alarm, the signal vanished.

explanationZhTw: 表示「沒來上班／缺勤」，不是單純「遺失」。
generatedExample: She was missing from work on Monday because she had a doctor’s appointment.

explanationZhTw: 指「對他的缺席發表意見／閒談」，這裡帶有八卦、冷漠的語氣。
generatedExample: People commented on her absence, but nobody called her.

explanationZhTw: 建築物入口附近的前廳或門廳；這個詞比一般的「hall」更正式、較少見。
generatedExample: We waited in the vestibule until the rain stopped.

explanationZhTw: 公告欄、布告板，用來張貼訊息或通知。
generatedExample: The schedule was pinned to the notice-board near the door.

explanationZhTw: 把文字劃掉，表示刪除或作廢。
generatedExample: He crossed out the wrong answer and wrote a new one.

explanationZhTw: 不是字面上的「停止存在」而已，而是強烈表達他已被完全抹除，像從世界上消失。
generatedExample: When the company closed, the old brand ceased to exist.

explanationZhTw: 「baking hot」是慣用說法，表示「熱得像在烤箱裡一樣，非常炎熱」。
generatedExample: It was baking hot outside, so we stayed indoors.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「labyrinthine」不是字面上的「迷宮」，而是指事物「錯綜複雜、難以理解或辨認方向」。
generatedExample: The hospital has a labyrinthine layout, so visitors often get lost.

explanationZhTw: 「in full swing」表示某件事正如火如荼地進行中，已進入最忙、最活躍的階段。
generatedExample: The festival was in full swing when we arrived.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「加班、超時工作」，也就是比正常工時工作更久。
generatedExample: She has been working overtime all week to finish the report.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的片語動詞「taken off」表示「被調離、被移出原本的工作或任務」。
generatedExample: He was taken off the project after the deadline slipped again.

explanationZhTw: 「rushing out」表示趕工快速做出或匆忙推出某物。
generatedExample: The team was rushing out a quick update before the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「back files」指舊檔案、過往存檔資料，這裡是指以前的報紙或文件紀錄。
generatedExample: She checked the back files to find the original article.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「embellishing」不是單純「裝飾」，而是指「潤飾、誇飾，甚至加添讓內容看起來更好的細節」。
generatedExample: He embellished the story to make it sound more impressive.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「febrile」不是醫學上的「發燒」，而是形容氣氛「亢奮、躁動、緊繃」。
generatedExample: The room had a febrile energy before the exam results were announced.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `plugged` 不是「插上」的意思，而是「反覆宣傳、強力推銷」。`endlessly` 強調是一直不停地播放和灌輸。
generatedExample: The ad was endlessly plugged on social media until everyone knew the brand name.

explanationZhTw: `take a fancy to` 是慣用語，表示「喜歡上、突然對某物產生好感」。
generatedExample: My little sister took a fancy to the red bicycle in the shop window.

explanationZhTw: `in his native element` 表示「如魚得水、在最適合自己的環境中」。
generatedExample: At the science fair, she was in her native element and answered every question confidently.

explanationZhTw: `as happy as a lark` 是比喻說法，表示「非常開心、心情很好」。
generatedExample: After the exam was over, he was as happy as a lark.

explanationZhTw: `pretext` 在這裡是「藉口、理由」，帶有不完全真誠的意味。
generatedExample: She used a family emergency as a pretext to leave the meeting early.

explanationZhTw: `jolly someone along` 表示「用輕鬆熱情的方式催促、鼓勵大家繼續做事」。
generatedExample: The coach kept jollying the team along when they started to lose energy.

explanationZhTw: 「foreshortening」是透視畫法的術語，指因視角關係，物體看起來被壓縮或縮短的效果。
generatedExample: The artist used foreshortening to make the hand seem closer to the viewer.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「plastered on」不是「抹上灰泥」，而是指到處張貼、滿滿貼在表面上。
generatedExample: Posters were plastered on every window in the station.

explanationZhTw: 此處的「outnumbering」表示數量多到超過別的東西。
generatedExample: The new signs were outnumbering the old ones after the renovation.

explanationZhTw: 「apathetic」表示冷淡、無動於衷，對事情沒有興趣或情緒反應。
generatedExample: She felt apathetic about the election and did not follow the news.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「lashed into」表示被強烈煽動、逼得進入某種情緒或行動狀態。
generatedExample: The speech lashed the crowd into a sudden burst of shouting.

explanationZhTw: 「frenzies」指狂熱、失控的情緒爆發或集體亢奮。
generatedExample: Fans went into frenzies when the band walked on stage.

explanationZhTw: 「burned in effigy」是以焚燒象徵物來表達抗議或仇恨。
generatedExample: The mayor was burned in effigy after the scandal broke.

explanationZhTw: 「torn down」在這裡是指被撕下、拆掉或移除。
generatedExample: The old posters were torn down before the campaign began.

explanationZhTw: 「flew round」表示消息、謠言很快傳開。
generatedExample: A rumor flew round the office before lunch.

explanationZhTw: 「foreign extraction」指外國出身或外國血統，帶有被標記身分的意味。
generatedExample: The family was treated suspiciously because of their foreign extraction.

explanationZhTw: 「set on fire」就是放火燒、使著火。
generatedExample: Someone set the shed on fire during the night.

explanationZhTw: 「perished」是較正式的說法，表示因災難、窒息等而死亡。
generatedExample: Several animals perished in the flood.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，指「像天堂一樣舒服、完美的地方」，和前文的骯髒形成反差。
generatedExample: After the long hike, the shady lake felt like paradise.

explanationZhTw: 「黑市」；指未經合法管道、常常違法買賣的市場。
generatedExample: They bought the phone on the black market.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「迅速脫掉」或「用力扯掉」。
generatedExample: He tore off his coat and jumped into the water.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「表達愛意」，而是指「發生性行為」。
generatedExample: The novel describes two characters who make love in secret.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純的「集合」，而是指「恢復力量、重新振作、重新集結」。
generatedExample: After the pause, the team rallied and played better.

explanationZhTw: 指「大量聚集、集結成群」；這裡把昆蟲寫得像軍隊一樣。
generatedExample: People were massing outside the stadium before the game.

explanationZhTw: 「反攻、反擊」；對對方的攻勢作出回擊。
generatedExample: The army launched a counter-attack at dawn.

explanationZhTw: 片語，表示「用最大音量、大聲地」；這裡指衝著電幕大吼。
generatedExample: He called my name at the top of his voice from the other side of the street.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，表示「困難、苦差事、難熬的事」；文中是指只能偶爾見面也不算太痛苦。
generatedExample: For her, waking up early every day was a hardship at first.

explanationZhTw: 副詞，表示「不常、很少地」。
generatedExample: We only meet infrequently these days because everyone is so busy.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞，表示「未被侵犯的、完好無損的、保持原樣的」；這裡強調房間仍然安全完整。
generatedExample: The old manuscript was kept inviolate in a climate-controlled room.

explanationZhTw: 比喻說法，指一小塊被保存下來的舊時代空間，不是字面上的「口袋」。
generatedExample: That café is a pocket of the past in a neighborhood full of new buildings.

explanationZhTw: 比喻說法，指生活得像幽靈一樣，幾乎不與外界接觸、非常隱晦。
generatedExample: After the scandal, he lived a ghostlike existence and avoided everyone.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 stock 指店裡的貨品、存貨；worthless stock 就是「沒什麼價值的貨」。
generatedExample: The shop was cluttered with worthless stock that nobody wanted to buy.

explanationZhTw: 指熱情已經變淡、顯得勉強而不再鮮明。
generatedExample: She spoke with faded enthusiasm, as if the project no longer excited her.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，表示「費力地找出、翻出、掏出」；這裡是從記憶深處挖出零碎童謠。
generatedExample: He dragged out an old photo album from the closet.

explanationZhTw: 動詞，表示「回想起、記起」；比 remember 稍正式。
generatedExample: I can recall her face, but not her name.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「這件事一直掛在他們心上／從未被忘記」。
generatedExample: The deadline was never out of her minds that week.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「死亡即將逼近」這件事，強調那種迫在眉睫的壓迫感。
generatedExample: The family could not ignore the fact of impending death.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 sanctuary 不是宗教場所，而是「避難所、讓人安心的安全地方」。
generatedExample: For her, the small library was sanctuary from the noise outside.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「放任自己沉浸於」某種想法或活動中。
generatedExample: After dinner, he gave himself up to reading for an hour.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 hold 指「持續維持、一直有效」；整句是說他們的運氣會一直維持下去。
generatedExample: We hoped the weather would hold indefinitely, but it started raining again.

explanationZhTw: carry on 表示「繼續」；intrigue 在這裡是「秘密關係／私情」。整體意思是繼續他們的秘密關係。
generatedExample: They decided to carry on their intrigue in secret.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「把自己改頭換面到別人認不出來」。
generatedExample: The witness altered himself out of recognition before leaving the city.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「度過他們的餘生」。
generatedExample: They planned to live out their lives near the coast.

explanationZhTw: 「take the first step」指先踏出第一步、開始採取行動；在這裡是說他們不知道該怎麼開始反抗。
generatedExample: The hardest part of learning a language is often taking the first step and speaking out loud.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「找到路走進去」，而是指找到進入某個組織的門路或途徑。
generatedExample: She spent weeks finding her way into the local volunteer network.

explanationZhTw: 「strike someone as」表示「讓某人覺得／看起來像」，強調主觀看法，不是客觀事實。
generatedExample: His silence struck me as a little unusual.

explanationZhTw: 「on the strength of」意思是「憑藉、根據」，這裡指只憑一瞬間的眼神就相信對方。
generatedExample: He got the job on the strength of his interview and portfolio.

explanationZhTw: 「take it for granted」是「想當然地認為、理所當然地接受」；這裡表示她沒有懷疑這個想法。
generatedExample: I took it for granted that the meeting was on Friday, but it was actually on Thursday.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「rubbish」不是「垃圾」字面意思，而是口語中的「胡說八道、廢話」。
generatedExample: That's rubbish; I never said anything like that.

explanationZhTw: 「at the top of her voice」表示「用最大音量、聲嘶力竭地」喊叫。
generatedExample: He called my name at the top of his voice from across the street.

explanationZhTw: 「the dimmest idea」表示「幾乎完全不知道、一點概念都沒有」；這裡強調她對 Goldstein 幾乎沒有認識。
generatedExample: I had the dimmest idea where the station was, so I asked for directions.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是說某件事超出她能想像或理解的範圍，不是字面上的「在想像外面」。
generatedExample: A life without the internet is outside my imagination now.

explanationZhTw: 「invincible」指「不可戰勝的、無敵的」；在這裡是她認為政黨永遠不會被打倒。
generatedExample: They looked invincible after winning ten games in a row.

explanationZhTw: 「at most」表示「頂多、至多」，用來限制前面說法的上限。
generatedExample: The trip will take two hours at most.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `acute` 不是「尖銳」的字面意思，而是指思考敏銳、判斷力強。
generatedExample: She is acute enough to notice when something is wrong.

explanationZhTw: `susceptible to` 表示「容易受到……影響」；這裡是容易被宣傳影響。
generatedExample: Teenagers can be susceptible to peer pressure.

explanationZhTw: `propaganda` 指帶有操控目的的宣傳，不是一般中性的「廣告」或「資訊」。
generatedExample: The article warned readers to question political propaganda.

explanationZhTw: `burst out laughing` 是慣用說法，表示突然大笑出來。
generatedExample: He tried to stay serious, but he burst out laughing.

explanationZhTw: `touch upon` 表示「稍微提到、涉及到」；不是真的碰到。
generatedExample: The speaker touched upon the budget problem briefly.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `mythology` 不是神話故事，而是指官方刻意編造、反覆灌輸的說法。
generatedExample: The regime promoted its own official mythology about the past.

explanationZhTw: `falsehood` 指「虛假、謊言」；比 `lie` 更正式。
generatedExample: He was accused of spreading falsehoods online.

explanationZhTw: `sham` 在這裡是「假象、假貨、騙局」的意思，表示這場戰爭並非真實正常。
generatedExample: The agreement turned out to be a sham.

explanationZhTw: `switchover` 指「切換、轉變、改變到另一種狀態」；這裡是敵對對象的更換。
generatedExample: There was a sudden switchover in the company’s strategy.

explanationZhTw: `dimly recall` 表示「隱約想起」；記憶不清楚、很模糊。
generatedExample: I dimly recall seeing him at school years ago.

explanationZhTw: `one ... after another` 是慣用結構，表示「一個接一個、沒完沒了」；`bloody` 在這裡是帶情緒的強調語氣。
generatedExample: It was one mistake after another all morning.

explanationZhTw: 「impudent」指「無禮的、厚顏的」；這裡形容這些偽造行為帶有明目張膽、冒犯權威的意味。
generatedExample: His impudent remark made the whole room go silent.

explanationZhTw: 「forgeries」是「偽造品、偽造文件」；這裡指被捏造出來的假資料或假文書。
generatedExample: The museum discovered that the painting was a forgery.

explanationZhTw: 「horrify」是「使驚恐、使厭惡」；這裡表示這些事並沒有讓她感到震驚或反感。
generatedExample: The news horrified everyone in the town.

explanationZhTw: 「abyss」原意是「深淵」；這裡是比喻用法，指極深的危機、絕望或精神上的墜落感。
generatedExample: He stared into the abyss and felt a wave of panic.

explanationZhTw: 「momentous」是「重大的、意義深遠的」；這裡強調那張紙具有關鍵性的歷史或政治意義。
generatedExample: The agreement was a momentous step toward peace.

explanationZhTw: 在「make much impression on her」裡，impression 表示「影響、觸動」；不是單純的「印象」。
generatedExample: My speech did not make much impression on the audience.

explanationZhTw: 「grasp the point」中的「grasp」不是「抓住」，而是「理解、領會」；這是文中較特殊的用法。
generatedExample: It took me a while to grasp the meaning of the poem.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `know` 不是單純「知道」，而是指「認識、熟悉某人」；整句是在說他不認識那些人。
generatedExample: I never knew him well, only from a few meetings.

explanationZhTw: `besides` 這裡是接續補充，意思是「再說；而且；此外」。
generatedExample: I don't want to go, and besides, I'm too tired.

explanationZhTw: `the old days` 是慣用說法，指「過去的年代、往昔」。
generatedExample: In the old days, people wrote letters instead of texting.

explanationZhTw: `by sight` 表示「只是看過外貌而認得；眼熟但不熟識」。
generatedExample: I know her by sight, but we've never spoken.

explanationZhTw: 「worry about」表示「擔心、掛念」。這裡是說既然人們一直在被殺，那還有什麼好擔心的，帶有反問語氣。
generatedExample: I worry about my sister when she works late at night.

explanationZhTw: 「kill off」是片語動詞，意思是「大量殺死、消滅」。這裡用被動式，強調人們正一個個被殺掉。
generatedExample: The disease killed off most of the fish in the lake.

explanationZhTw: 「all the time」表示「一直、總是、持續不斷地」。這裡用來強調事情發生得非常頻繁。
generatedExample: He checks his phone all the time during meetings.

explanationZhTw: 指「特例、特殊情況」。這裡是在強調這件事不能用一般標準看待。
generatedExample: This is an exceptional case, so the usual rules do not apply.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般的「廢除法律」而已，而是指「把過去從記錄與存在中抹掉」。
generatedExample: The old system was abolished overnight.

explanationZhTw: 指「實體物件、具體可觸摸的東西」。這裡用來對比沒有文字紀錄的抽象記憶。
generatedExample: The museum keeps the old tools as solid objects from daily life.

explanationZhTw: 「幾乎一無所知」；literally 在這裡用來強調程度，表示幾乎完全沒有資訊。
generatedExample: I literally know nothing about that topic.

explanationZhTw: 指「篡改、偽造」。在這裡特別是把歷史紀錄改得不真實。
generatedExample: Someone falsified the report to hide the mistake.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是帶有政治隱喻的說法，指「只有永遠當下、沒有過去與未來的狀態」。
generatedExample: In a crisis, it can feel like an endless present with no time to plan ahead.

explanationZhTw: 指「證據、跡證」。這裡強調事情做完後，能證明它發生過的線索也被清掉了。
generatedExample: There was no evidence left at the scene.

explanationZhTw: 指「具體而明確的證據」，不是模糊印象或推測。
generatedExample: We need concrete evidence before we accuse anyone.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是「事情發生之後、事後」。
generatedExample: He explained the decision after the event.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思是「那有什麼用／那有什麼意義？」；`what good` 在這種語境中不是單純指「多好」，而是用來質疑某件事是否有實際價值。
generatedExample: You studied all night, but what good was that if you forgot the formula during the exam?

explanationZhTw: 「no good」在這裡表示「沒用、沒有價值、不值得保留」，是常見口語說法。
generatedExample: The old receipt is no good now, so I can throw it away.

explanationZhTw: 「throw away」是片語動詞，意思是「丟掉、丟棄」。這裡的 it 指前面提到的東西。
generatedExample: She threw away the broken box after lunch.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「keep」是「保留、不丟掉」的意思；在這個語境中，重點是把東西留下來而不是處理掉。
generatedExample: If I found that note again, I would keep it in my wallet.

explanationZhTw: 冒險；願意承擔風險。
generatedExample: She is willing to take risks if the reward is worth it.

explanationZhTw: 值得的；有價值的，表示花時間、精力或冒險去做是划算的。
generatedExample: The trip was worth while because we learned so much.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思是「拿它來做什麼／如何使用它」，不是字面上的「做完它」。
generatedExample: What could you have done with that old key?

explanationZhTw: 「evidence」是「證據、佐證」；這裡指能支持前文判斷的線索。
generatedExample: The new report was strong evidence of fraud.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，表示「在別人心中埋下些許懷疑」。
generatedExample: Her silence planted a few doubts in my mind.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是隱喻，指一小團、一小股反抗力量，不是字面上的「結」。
generatedExample: Small knots of resistance formed after the new policy was announced.

explanationZhTw: 指「突然出現、迅速冒出」，帶有生動的動態感。
generatedExample: New shops are springing up all over the street.

explanationZhTw: 指人們聯合起來、組成一個小團體共同行動。
generatedExample: The neighbors banded themselves together to protect the park.

explanationZhTw: 指「繼續下去、延續進行」。
generatedExample: Please carry on while I answer the phone.

explanationZhTw: 指「停止、暫停不做」；此處是談事情中斷的位置。
generatedExample: Let's leave off here and continue tomorrow.

explanationZhTw: 「對……有興趣／關心」；這裡是否定，表示「我對下一代沒興趣／不關心」。
generatedExample: She is interested in history, but not in politics.

explanationZhTw: 「世代；一代人」；指同一時間出生或長大的人群。
generatedExample: My generation grew up with mobile phones.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `US` 不是「美國」，而是口語裡的「我們」；大寫只是為了強調。
generatedExample: Don't ignore us when you make the decision.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是帶有隱喻與委婉語氣的說法，意思不是一般的「叛逆者」，而是指某人在性或身體欲望方面較放縱、較不守規矩。
generatedExample: He seemed disciplined in public, but he was a rebel from the waist downwards when it came to relationships.

explanationZhTw: 這裡 brilliantly 不是字面上的「閃亮地」，而是加強語氣，表示「非常、極其」；witty 指「機智而好笑的」。整個片語表示她覺得這件事或這句話很聰明、很有趣。
generatedExample: The joke was brilliantly witty, so everyone laughed immediately.

explanationZhTw: flung ... round him 在這裡不是單純「丟」手臂，而是指「突然把雙臂環抱住他」或「熱情地擁抱他」。round 在英式英文中等於 around。
generatedExample: When he came back home, she flung her arms round him.

explanationZhTw: 片語「not the faintest」表示「一點也不／絲毫不」，整句就是「一點興趣都沒有」。這是英語裡很常見的否定強調說法。
generatedExample: I had not the faintest interest in his argument.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是常見片語「fall asleep」，意思是「睡著」。文中用進行式表示動作正在發生。
generatedExample: She kept falling asleep during the long meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「orthodoxy」不是一般宗教上的「正統」，而是指「符合官方思想、被體制接受的立場」。在這種政治語境裡，含義比較專門。
generatedExample: In that system, orthodoxy meant repeating the approved line.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「orthodoxy」不是一般宗教上的「正統」，而是指「符合官方思想、被體制接受的立場」。在這種政治語境裡，含義比較專門。
generatedExample: In that system, orthodoxy meant repeating the approved line.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「施加」，而是「自然滲透、強勢地作用在某人身上」的意思。可理解為某種觀念不知不覺地壓到人們身上。
generatedExample: The new routine imposed itself on everyone at the office.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「吞下」食物，而是比喻為「全盤接受、照單全收」。也就是沒有懷疑就相信一切。
generatedExample: He swallowed everything the rumor said without checking it.
